title: Forge Method workflow definition
description: Schema for a Forge Method agent-facing workflow reference. A workflow is a compact state machine with trigger, inputs, steps, outputs, done_when, blocked_when, and handoff. Draft 2020-12.

id.description: Workflow identifier. Lowercase, kebab-case, matches the reference filename (e.g. 'build-story').

trigger.description: When this workflow fires. Either a single condition or a list of conditions that should all hold.

inputs.description: Inputs the workflow consumes. Each input names what is needed and whether it is required.

name.description: Name of the input (artifact path, state field, or described resource).

required.description: Whether the workflow cannot proceed without this input.

steps.description: Ordered list of step descriptions. Order is significant; each entry is a single mechanical or decision step.

outputs.description: What the workflow produces (artifacts, state updates, decisions).

done_when.description: Conditions that signal completion. All must hold for the workflow to be considered done.

blocked_when.description: Conditions that block progress and require a human decision or external input before continuing.

handoff.description: What to preserve when handing off (artifact paths, accepted assumptions, next action). Optional.
